name: Grandma Thorpe's Currant Pancakes
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together the flour and salt.
2. Add baking powder and shortening, cutting in as for biscuits.
3. Stir in currants.
4. Gradually add milk until a soft dough forms that can be rolled out.
5. Place on a floured surface and roll into thin pancakes.
6. Cook on a hot griddle until light brown.
7. Butter and sprinkle with sugar.
This recipe is from Blodwyn Thorpe, the daughter of Welsh immigrants born in Malad City, Idaho in the 1890s. She was the last of 13 children born to a pioneer Mormon family. Blodwyn's daughter, Neva, married Giovani Dominico Cambarari, an Italian immigrant, and they are the parents of Katherine Francis Cambareri Day, my lovely bride.
This recipe was never written down by Blodwyn and is somewhat inexact, as are most of the recipes we have from the Thorpe side of the family.
